compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
Make `idea` a first-class ticket status and keep it out of the automated
work-dispatch surfaces.

## Why

`idea` must exist as a legal status before any other part of the feature (gate
bypass, Jira mapping, the `idea` command) is meaningful. It also must never be
treated as dispatchable work: an idea is captured-but-undesigned, so it must not
surface in `ready` or `next-batch`.

Why a first-class status rather than a tag on an `open` ticket: a status gives
structural unclaimability. `claim_core` accepts only tickets whose status is
`open`, so an `idea`-status ticket is unreachable by the claim primitive with no
extra filtering — whereas a tagged-`open` ticket stays claimable via `claim <id>`
and leaves a genesis window where it is `open` before the tag is applied.

Prerequisite: this story is foundational — the other four child stories
(eced-c6b8-7461-48a0, 1982-bf75-0f28-4e91, 6173-32ea-1fc0-4f04,
2918-24c8-4c2a-4e10) depend on the `idea` status value existing, so this story
should land first.

## What

- Add `idea` to the canonical enum source `src/rebar/schemas/common.schema.json`
  (`$defs/ticket_status`), then regenerate the `TicketStatus` literal with
  `python -m rebar.schemas.gen_types` (the literal is a generated artifact guarded
  by a CI drift-gate — editing `types.py` by hand would fail the gate).
- Add `idea` to the transition validator so transitions into and out of `idea`
  are legal (no prohibited edges — clients drive their own workflow).
- Add `idea` to the accepted `list --status` filter set.
- Keep `idea` OUT of the "open-ish" sets that feed dispatch, so exclusion from
  `ready`/`next-batch` is achieved by omission, not by new filter code.
- Confirm `search` returns `idea` tickets (the search path is not status-filtered;
  add a regression test).
- Document the `idea` status AND its lifecycle in the committed `CLAUDE.md`:
  eligible transitions, the `idea → closed` reject/drop bypass, the `validate`
  exemptions, and the Jira `IDEA` mapping prerequisite.

## Scope

- `src/rebar/schemas/common.schema.json` — canonical `ticket_status` enum source.
- `src/rebar/types.py` — regenerated `TicketStatus` literal (via `gen_types`).
- `src/rebar/_commands/transition.py` — `_VALID_STATUSES`.
- `src/rebar/_engine_support/reads_cli.py` — `list --status` accepted set.
- `src/rebar/graph/_ready.py` — verify `idea` is absent from `_OPEN_STATUSES`.
- `src/rebar/_engine_support/next_batch.py` — verify `idea` is absent from the
  `open,in_progress` candidate filter.
- `CLAUDE.md` — status + full-lifecycle documentation.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `idea` is present in `common.schema.json` `$defs/ticket_status`.
- [ ] `python -m rebar.schemas.gen_types` regenerates `types.py` and the CI
      drift-gate passes with `idea` in the `TicketStatus` literal.
- [ ] `transition <id> open idea` succeeds with exit 0.
- [ ] `transition <id> idea open` succeeds with exit 0.
- [ ] `transition <id> idea in_progress` succeeds with exit 0.
- [ ] `rebar list --status=idea` returns tickets whose status is `idea`.
- [ ] `rebar list --status=idea` excludes tickets whose status is not `idea`.
- [ ] `rebar ready` never returns a ticket whose status is `idea` (test-verified).
- [ ] `rebar next-batch <epic>` never returns a ticket whose status is `idea`
      (test-verified).
- [ ] `rebar search <term>` returns a matching `idea` ticket (test-verified).
- [ ] The committed `CLAUDE.md` documents the `idea` status and its lifecycle
      (transitions, close-bypass, validate exemptions, Jira prerequisite).
- [ ] The full test suite passes with all five sibling changes integrated (this
      foundational story owns the integrated-suite check).
- [ ] `make lint` passes for the changed files.

file_impact.reason: canonical ticket_status enum source — add idea here first
keep idea out of the candidate filter

compiled_state.title: Add 'idea' as a first-class status excluded from ready/next-batch
